ticket.opened.title: 🎫 Ticket Opened
ticket.opened.description: Thanks for reaching out, {user}! A staff member will be with you shortly.
ticket.opened.description.new: Your ticket has been opened for **{category}**.
ticket.opened.field.subject: Subject
ticket.closed.title: 🔒 Ticket Closed
ticket.closed.channelMessage:
🔒 This ticket was closed by {user}.
Reason: {reason}
ticket.closed.channelMessage.deleting: This channel will be deleted in a few seconds.
ticket.closed.alreadyClosed.title: ℹ️ Already Closed
ticket.closed.alreadyClosed.description: This ticket has already been closed.
ticket.closed.notFound.title: ❌ Ticket Not Found
ticket.closed.notFound.description: No open ticket was found for you in this server.
ticket.closed.forbidden.title: ⛔ Forbidden
ticket.closed.forbidden.description: Only the ticket opener or staff can close this ticket.
ticket.claim.alreadyClaimed.title: ❌ Already Claimed
ticket.claim.alreadyClaimed.description: This ticket is already claimed by {user}.
ticket.claim.notClaimed.title: ❌ Not Claimed
ticket.claim.notClaimed.description: This ticket is not currently claimed.
ticket.claim.forbidden.title: ⛔ Forbidden
ticket.claim.forbidden.description: Only staff can claim or unclaim tickets.
ticket.claim.noTicketHere.title: ❌ No Ticket Here
ticket.claim.noTicketHere.description: This command must be used inside an open ticket channel.
ticket.new.categoryNotFound.title: ❌ Category Not Found
ticket.new.categoryNotFound.description: A category named {category} could not be found.
guild.required.title: ❌ Guild Required
guild.required.description: This command can only be used inside a server.
error.generic: An unexpected error occurred while processing that request.
command.unknown: That slash command is not registered by this scaffold.
language.updated.title: 🌐 Language Updated
language.invalid.title: ❌ Unsupported Language
language.invalid.description: **{locale}** isn't a supported language code. Supported languages: {supported}.
help.faq.language.setQuestion: How do I set the server language?
help.faq.language.setAnswer: Run `/language set locale:<code>` with a locale code, e.g. `en` for English or `es` for Spanish. It applies to the whole server, not per-user.
help.faq.language.supportedAnswer: Currently supported: {supported}. More languages are on the way.
